symptoms: executor: fix unstable test in the TestPointGetLockExistKey
failure_signature: executor: fix unstable test in the TestPointGetLockExistKey
root_cause_explanation: The test coordinates two concurrent sessions (pessimistic locking + writes) using goroutines and fixed `time.Sleep(...)` delays, which can be timing-sensitive under CI load. This PR improves synchronization by waiting for concurrent goroutines to finish (e.g., using `WaitGroup` wrappers) instead of relying on channel ordering alone, reducing async timing races in the test.
fix_pattern: Replace fragile goroutine/channel coordination with explicit synchronization (e.g., `WaitGroup`) and avoid relying solely on fixed sleeps when asserting lock/write ordering in concurrent tests.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix unstable test in the TestPointGetLockExistKey
